matches.match_rationale: Exam timetabling across 10-15 campuses is a classic constraint satisfaction problem: room capacities, proctor availability, student cohort clashes, exam window limits, and inter-campus paper distribution timelines must all be satisfied simultaneously. Existing manual spreadsheet approaches break down at this scale, and cascading last-minute changes require rapid re-solving across all constraints. This is precisely the domain where constraint solvers with AI-assisted configuration yield dramatic operational improvements.
matches.opportunity_sketch: An AI-driven timetabling tool that ingests room capacities, staff availability, student enrolments, and exam windows per campus, then generates conflict-free schedules and propagates last-minute changes automatically across all sites.

matches.match_rationale: Proctor assignment and room allocation across 10-15 campuses require optimally matching limited staff (proctors) and rooms to time slots under capacity and availability constraints. The pain of staff overtime and last-minute gaps is a direct resource allocation failure. AI scheduling tools can balance proctor workloads, flag under-resourced sessions, and suggest reallocation when changes occur.
matches.opportunity_sketch: An automated proctor and room scheduling module that allocates staff and spaces across campuses, flags overtime risks, and re-optimises assignments when exam windows shift.

matches.match_rationale: The end-to-end exam logistics workflow — generating timetables, notifying campus coordinators, tracking paper distribution, sending confirmation requests, and logging changes — is a multi-step, multi-system process that currently relies on manual email chains and phone calls. A task automation agent can orchestrate these steps across the organisation's systems, reducing coordinator overhead and ensuring no site is missed when changes cascade.
matches.opportunity_sketch: An agentic workflow that, triggered by a timetable change, automatically notifies affected campus coordinators, updates the central exam calendar, re-assigns impacted proctors, and logs the change trail — without manual follow-up.

matches.match_rationale: Exam administration generates significant communication overhead — briefing packs for proctors, change notifications to campuses, secure handling instructions, and last-minute memos. At 10-15 campuses this volume is non-trivial, and inconsistent communications create compliance risk. LLM text generation can draft standardised, context-specific communications rapidly from structured exam data.
matches.opportunity_sketch: An automated communication generator that produces proctor briefings, campus notification emails, and change advisories from the exam schedule data, ensuring consistency and reducing coordinator drafting time.
